local_setup_divergence.notes: Used an isolated default-branch worktree and the exact Factory PR #329 verifier commit.

roadmap_reconciliation.notes: MCP #749 is the validated release-delivery lane. This PR changes only trusted default-branch release merge control.

state_refinement.release_state: No canonical product lifecycle behavior changed. Repository-local release control now derives merge eligibility from a complete exact assignment set plus every declared required topology receipt.
invariants: Canonical product state, authority, and persistence semantics did not change.
Atlas may append evidence and set pending, but only trusted default-branch CI may set release-unit / merge-eligible success.
Topology-optional Release Units become eligible after exact assignment; topology-required units remain pending until observed exact receipts verify.
Merge eligibility does not prove publication, deployment, served identity, or product acceptance.

rejected_or_collapsed_states: Collapsed assignment and topology completeness into one authoritative merge-eligibility result while retaining per-unit topology evidence.
Kept release publication and deployment outside the PR merge gate.
minimization_review.rationale: One consolidated merge status is the smallest honest protected-branch surface; assignment and topology remain distinct evidence inputs.

domain_entity_candidates: None. Release Unit, Release Assignment, topology receipt, and merge eligibility are Factory-owned terms.
factory_learning_signals: An assignment-only protected status cannot enforce a Release Unit whose proposal declares required topology.

validation_evidence.syntax: Workflow YAML parses and git diff whitespace validation passes.
validation_evidence.trust: Caller pins one exact Factory commit and a repository-scoped main-only read identity.

end_of_session_capture.notes: Factory #329 supplies the consolidated assignment-plus-topology resolver.

session: Install consolidated Release Unit merge eligibility resolver
